rows.title: Angie Pate
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- Jackson's mom. FNP (Family Nurse Practitioner).
- Works at Rainbow City Family Medicine.
- Deeply appreciated by Jackson.

## Context
- Tends to enable Cameron (Jackson's brother) — a source of friction in Jackson's view.
- Jackson's relationship with his family is complicated ideologically, but his appreciation for his mom is genuine.

## Significant Moments

### October 2025 — Calling Her When Things Went Wrong
During a particularly rough stretch of his fall semester, Jackson had a late-night situation spiral: he went to retrieve his phone from Ella Dupree's apartment, broke her toes in the chaos, and drove home panicked. His first call was to his mom. She told him he was in the right. That landed more than just reassurance — it was the specific comfort that only she provides: unconditional backing when he's spiraling and doubting himself. He noted it in his journal the next day, specifically that it made him feel better about the situation.

### October 1, 2025 — Missing Her During the Dark Stretch
While Jackson was throwing his weed out and trying to turn his life around at the start of his sophomore year, he wrote about missing his mom in his journal — unprompted, in the middle of a passage about loneliness and isolation. "She works a lot and I am so thankful for all she does for me." It's a small line, but it shows up during one of the lowest points of his fall: he wasn't reaching out to friends in those entries, he was missing her. The appreciation was quiet but genuine.

### January 2026 — "Love You. Be Careful."
A late-night Snapchat from her: "Love you. Be careful." Simple and brief, but she sends it unprompted. Their Snapchat dynamic is light — mostly photos, a few short messages — and yet she consistently shows up. It's the kind of small, steady parental contact that doesn't make headlines but holds the thread. Jackson keeps her as a Snapchat friend under "Mom" (username dabombmom1000), which in itself says something.

## Mentioned In

### November 8, 2025 — referenced in Jackson's journal after the Ella Dupree incident
After the bar night that ended with him accidentally breaking Ella Dupree's toes, Jackson drove home panicked and called his mom. She told him he was in the right. He noted it in his journal the next day specifically — not just that he talked to her, but that it made him feel better about the situation. During a stretch when Jackson was isolated and struggling, she was the person he defaulted to when things went sideways.
Source: **2025-11-08-1**, **Ella Dupree**

### October 1, 2025 — mentioned unprompted in journal during dark stretch
Mid-entry about loneliness and throwing his weed out, Jackson wrote: "I miss my mom — she works a lot and I am so thankful for all she does for me." No context, no lead-up — just said it. He wasn't writing about family, he was writing about being alone, and she came up anyway. That's what it was.
Source: **2025-10-01-1**

### June 2022 — mentioned by Jackson in conversation with **Bailee**
At 3am, Jackson texted Bailee: "mother angie doesnt not like you anymore." Bailee reacted with shock ("OMG FR?!") and Jackson confirmed it was a "big surprise." A few days later Bailee sent: "i was depressed" — suggesting she understood why Angie's opinion had shifted, referencing a hard period she'd been going through. Reveals that Angie formed opinions about Jackson's friends and communicated them to him, and that at least at one point she'd withdrawn approval of Bailee. Jackson used the affectionate "mother angie" phrasing even while delivering uncomfortable news.
Source: **Bailee**, **baileeneal1**

### December 14, 2023 — Surgery
Jackson skipped bowling practice to go to the hospital: "mom has to have a surgery." He texted his teammates at 3:17 PM to explain why he wasn't coming. No detail on what the surgery was. The fact that he went to the hospital rather than sending her alone — and that it was the first reason he offered — tracks with how much she anchors him.
Source: **bowling**

### Spring 2026 — workplace central to academic integrity case
Angie works at Rainbow City Family Medicine — the same clinic that generated the excuse note at the center of Jackson's academic integrity hearing. She was explicitly uninvolved: "My mother works at Rainbow City Family Medicine but was not involved in my visit, scheduling, or documentation in any way." Jackson flagged this preemptively in his defense to avoid any appearance of conflict of interest, and directed the committee to verify through other clinic staff. Her employer becoming a contested institution in his academic defense — while she had no role in it — is a notable piece of context about how close her professional world sits to his.
Source: **academic-integrity-defense**, **Ivonne Joiner**

rows.title: Anna
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- Active friend. Consistent Snapchat contact for 5+ years (Sep 2020 → Dec 2025).
- No additional biographical detail confirmed.

## Snapchat
- **Username:** atallent97 (display: "anna")
- **Messages:** 433 (Sep 2020 → Dec 2025) — 6th highest all-time
- **Snaps:** 84
- **Friend status:** Active
- **Note:** One of the longest sustained connections in Jackson's social network. Consistent volume over 5+ years without any clear spike or fade — a stable, low-drama presence.

## Significant Moments

### December 2021 — The Readd After 31 Weeks
Anna had been dating Ethan, and during the relationship she'd deleted Jackson from Snapchat because he didn't allow her to talk to guy friends. When they broke up, she readded Jackson immediately. Jackson called it out directly — "why the readd after 31 weeks?" She was straight with him: she wasn't allowed to have guy friends, and now that was over. No drama, no deflection. It was one of those moments that revealed something about her situation she'd been living in, and she handled it with a quiet kind of dignity — "you live and you learn." Jackson respected that.

### May 2022 — She Talked Him Down
One night Jackson texted Anna saying life was making him suicidal. She didn't panic or brush it off. She came back with faith — told him God doesn't take anyone through hardships He knows they can't survive, that even Christ suffered, and that hard times are part of it. She wasn't performative about it. Jackson pushed back a little, said he'd heard it all before, but she didn't flinch — just kept steady. Then she asked what was actually wrong. He said he was fine. She let it land and told him she was there if he needed her, a hug included. She was 16 or 17 at the time. That kind of groundedness from a friend that young is not nothing.

### April 2022 — "I Love You Too, Jackson"
In the middle of what appeared to be a running warm exchange one night, Jackson sent "i love you" and Anna came right back with "i love you too jackson." Stated plainly, no weirdness. With 5+ years of consistent contact and zero drama, that kind of easy warmth is what defines the friendship — not highs and lows, just reliability.

## Mentioned In (Additional)

### April 2022 — Anna in the "no balz" Group Chat
Anna (atallent97) was present in a small group chat called "no balz" (April 24–26, 2022) alongside Jackson. Jackson said "5 leaf clover" and Anna responded "that's fye / where is that." Minimal content, but it places Anna in a group context with Jackson in spring 2022 — around the same time as other key exchanges documented on her page (the May 2022 suicidal ideation conversation where she talked him down). They were in regular contact, group and direct.
Source: **no-balz**

### September–October 2025 — Anna in "🙄🙄🙄" Group Chat
Anna (atallent97) was the sole sender in a two-message group chat named "🙄🙄🙄" (Sept 20, 2025 and Oct 12, 2025) — both were snaps. The group name implies a shared frustration or running eye-roll about something. Jackson was in the group. No text content is visible beyond the snaps, but the chat's existence (and name) suggests Anna was in a mutual context with Jackson during fall semester 2025 at Auburn, still active in a group format.
Source: **🙄🙄🙄**

## Mentioned In

### April 2021 — Friend Admitted to Nearly Kissing Her
In Jackson's chat with mr.pandasempie (a high school-era friend), he received this message on April 18, 2021: "I just told Anna I was holding back the urge to kiss her 😂😂😂😂" — unprompted, sent to Jackson as if he'd confess it to him specifically. Jackson's response was just a photo. This was during the period when Anna and Ethan were dating (the relationship that ended in December 2021 with the "readd after 31 weeks" moment). The message reveals at least one person in Jackson's circle had feelings for Anna that went beyond friendship — something she may or may not have known. Context: mr.pandasempie appears to be a mutual friend from Jackson's high school soccer circle.
Source: **mr.pandasempie**

### 2025 — Ethan's Pattern Confirmed by **Ethan Arce**'s Snapchat with Jackson
Anna's page already documents that Ethan deleted her from Snapchat during their relationship because he didn't allow her to have guy friends (December 2021). Ethan's Snapchat conversation with Jackson across 2025 reveals the same pattern continued into a later relationship — he refers to someone named "Haven" repeatedly (April, June, and August 2025), with messages like "Fuck this is actually making me miss my haven fr" (April 7) and "What I said to haven btw" (April 24), and in August Jackson quotes Ethan saying "when we thought havens mom was gonna come in." The references suggest another serious relationship with the same possessive undertones. This is not directly about Anna but it corroborates the controlling behavior she experienced — it wasn't a one-time thing with her specifically. What happened to Anna appears to be a pattern.
Source: **Ethan Arce**, **ethan_arce14**

rows.title: Aubrie
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- Ex-girlfriend. Almost 2 years together.
- Major heartbreak — ended with false accusations.

## What She Left Behind
- Promise ring — Jackson still wears it daily. Origin kept private from others.

## Status
- Over. No contact.

## Snapchat
- **Username:** aubrieee.kate (displayed as "ak8")
- **Messages:** 681 (Oct 2022 → Nov 2024)
- **Snaps:** 991 — highest 1:1 snap count in the entire dataset
- **Friend status:** Deleted (Jackson removed her — aligns with "over, no contact")
- **Note:** The relationship's intensity shows clearly in the data. The snap volume (991) signals daily visual communication throughout the relationship period.
- **Relationship Map**

## Snapchat Intelligence
- **Username:** aubrieee.kate
- **Snaps:** Not in snap export (or not tracked)
- **Messages:** 681 (Oct 2022 -- Nov 2024) -- #3 all-time by messages
- **Pattern:** Chat-dominant relationship. No snap data = snaps weren't saved or relationship was text-first.
- **Era:** Auburn era, Oct 2022 -- Nov 2024. Aligns with the relationship timeline.
- **Status:** Over. No contact.
- **aubrieee.kate**

## Significant Moments

### Oct 2022–Nov 2024 — Almost Two Years
The relationship ran from fall of his freshman year at Auburn through the fall of his junior year. Nearly two years, and by volume the most active 1:1 dynamic in his entire Snapchat history — 991 snaps, 681 messages. That density signals something: this wasn't passive. They were in daily contact, consistently, for the full run of the relationship.

### Nov 23, 2024 — What He Said Right Before the End
A week before the conversation goes cold, Jackson sent her a message that reads like someone laying everything down at once: "you are the only person that i have ever seen myself in... i see our future, i see having kids with you... i want to be there every step of the way... i love you more than life itself." She responded — "I love you so much and that genuinely means the world" — and talked about wanting to be healthy together. Two days later she was still saying "I need you / No we aren't done." The conversation ends at November 24, 2024. Whatever broke them apart happened right after that.

### Nov 2024 — The End: False Accusations
The relationship ended with accusations Jackson considers false. What exactly she accused him of isn't documented here — that's deliberate, or unspoken, or both. He removed her from his Snapchat. She isn't in his friends list. The detail that sticks is that one week before this, they were still saying "I love you more than life itself" to each other. Whatever happened at the end hit hard enough to make two years feel finished.

### The Promise Ring
He still wears the ring she gave him — every day. He doesn't explain where it came from to other people. Keeps the origin private. That's not an accident. It's the only externally visible trace of the relationship that remained, and it's not something he makes easily explainable. That says something about how he held onto what the relationship was, even after what it became.

## Mentioned In

### June 2022 — Referenced as "ak" in Jackson's Chat with Brady
In Jackson's conversation with mrbradyt07 (Brady Tennyson) in June 2022, Jackson asked: "u gonna tell ak abt tre and nat" — "ak" being Aubrie's Snapchat display name ("ak8"). This places Aubrie in the social circle several months before she and Jackson began dating (Oct 2022). Whatever was happening between Trey and someone named "nat," Jackson believed Aubrie had standing to know about it — meaning she was already close enough to their world that information would matter to her. This is the earliest documented reference to Aubrie in Jackson's Snapchat record.
Source: **Trey Frachiseur**, **mrbradyt07**

### May 1, 2024 — Mentioned by **Tre Jackson** in conversation with Jackson
Tre texted Jackson at 2:37 PM: "Aubrie is in here." No additional context is captured — probably a location or group chat where she was present. The timing matters: May 2024 was within the active window of Jackson and Aubrie's relationship (Oct 2022–Nov 2024). Tre had enough awareness of Aubrie's presence to flag it to Jackson as noteworthy.
Source: **Tre Jackson**, **trej3635**

### July 2025 — mentioned by **Ella Dupree** in conversation with Jackson
During the blowup where Jackson confronted Ella Dupree about hooking up with his best friend, Ella Dupree had previously told Jackson "I'm not like Aubrie" — invoking Aubrie's name as a benchmark for untrustworthiness or betrayal. Jackson threw this back at her during the confrontation: "and 'im not like aubrie'" — meaning Ella Dupree had used Aubrie's name to position herself as more honest or loyal, and then did the exact thing that proved otherwise. Reveals that Aubrie's name had become shorthand in Jackson's social circle for a certain kind of relational betrayal. Whatever Aubrie did at the end of the relationship left enough of a reputation that others referenced her to signal trustworthiness.
Source: **Ella Dupree**, **elladupree22859**

rows.title: Bailee
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- Active friend. Primarily a snap-based connection.

## Snapchat
- **Username:** baileeneal1 (display: "Bailee")
- **Messages:** 81 (Mar 2022 → Apr 2026)
- **Snaps:** 316 — 80% of interactions are snaps
- **Friend status:** Active
- **Note:** Recent snap as of April 1, 2026 — still active.

## Significant Moments

### June 2022 — "Mother Angie Doesn't Like You Anymore"
Jackson texted Bailee late at night: "mother angie doesnt not like you anymore." Bailee responded with shock. Jackson said "big surprise" — so something had shifted with his mom's opinion of her. A few days later, Jackson sent: "always loved you / you left me / weve been through this." Whatever happened between them in that earlier period, this is evidence there was something real — a dynamic that ended, left some residue, and was apparently complicated enough that his mom formed a view on it.

### January 2025 — Giving Her Advice on Kody (After the Breakup)
Bailee came to Jackson after her breakup with Jax, processing feelings for Kody — someone she'd liked before the relationship. Jackson gave her real advice: don't act on it yet, give yourself actual time, not just a few weeks. He referenced his own pattern of moving too fast after Aubrie. He was honest enough to say he'd been through the same mistake himself, thoughtful enough not to just validate what she wanted to hear. It reveals him as someone she trusts for unfiltered perspective — and him as someone who takes that role seriously.

### June 2025 — The Court Hearing Update
Bailee sent Jackson a long, detailed account of going to court for a protective order against someone. She described standing before the judge, her father speaking up for her, the defendant claiming he didn't remember what happened, the judge offering to dismiss it in two months if no issues arose. Jackson's response was brief but steady: "you will always have me." She followed up: "I was just updating u on the situation." The intimacy of sharing that in detail — and his response being exactly what it needed to be without making it about him — captures what the friendship is at its core: she trusts him with things that matter.

### January 26, 2025 — The Pep Talk
Bailee was struggling — circumstances not specified — and Jackson sent an unprompted message about confidence, self-respect, and trusting God. "Just know that you're better than that, you're stronger mentally than that... act like you are doing great even if you aren't, that's gonna show him more than crying would." Then: "and don't break confidence." It's the kind of thing you say to someone you've known long enough to know exactly what they need to hear. He meant it.

## Mentioned In

### Feb–Apr 2023 — mentioned by Jackson in conversation with **Aubrie**
During fights with Aubrie, Jackson compared her behavior to Bailee's on at least three occasions: "that's bailee type shot" (Feb 12, 2023), "u sound like bailee rn" and "u act just like bailee sometimes" (Apr 17, 2023). Aubrie pushed back — "okay don't compare me to bailee" — suggesting Bailee carried a specific reputation in Jackson's eyes for a certain kind of behavior (reactive, difficult, or hot-headed). Jackson was using her as a negative reference point during relationship conflict. Reveals that however complicated the Jackson/Bailee dynamic was, she existed in his mind as a behavioral benchmark he didn't want Aubrie to emulate. The fact that Aubrie recognized the reference and objected to the comparison suggests Bailee's name and implied meaning were known context between them.
Source: **Aubrie**, **aubrieee.kate**

rows.title: Bailey Edwards
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- College-era romantic situation. Fully resolved April 2026.

## Known Details
- **Favorite color:** Purple
- **Flowers:** Daisies, hydrangeas, hibiscus
- **Family:** Twin brother named Ethan
- **Pets:** Has cats
- **Music:** Country music
- **Food:** Mexican food
- **Allergy:** Smoke allergy

## Timeline
- Prior hookup with Jansen (a distant friend of hers, ~1 year before reconnect — meaningless to Jackson, regretted it) caused her to drop him initially
- Reconnected at a Thanksgiving party (~Nov 2025)
- Kept a streak, conversations became daily and natural
- She went to Orlando and texted him the entire trip
- Said "I promise it's not washed, I just don't know what I want right now"
- Then cited "really different morals" and the Jansen situation as reasons
- Eventually ran its course

## How It Ended
- Jackson's final read: "she's the one who chose how she acted." Full emotional peace — no bitterness, no spiral.
- Said it was "probably the best I've ever felt emotionally" at the point of resolution.

## Other
- Rachel = her roommate

## Status
- Closed. No action needed.

## Significant Moments

### October 13, 2025 — First Mention
Jackson first noted Bailey in his journal on October 13, 2025. She appeared as someone he was beginning to notice — she was already on his radar. This is the earliest documented reference.

### October 19, 2025 — The Detailed Notes
Jackson wrote detailed notes about Bailey around October 19, 2025 — enough to document her preferences, her twin brother Ethan, her allergies, her tastes. The fact that he catalogued these details says something about how much attention he was paying.

### October 27, 2025 — She Went Distant
Around October 27, 2025, Bailey went distant on Jackson. She also posted something petty on Instagram around this time. He noticed both. The shift was sudden and unexplained.

### October 27, 2025 — Morning Rejection
At some point around this period, Bailey told Jackson he was "a shitty person." He hadn't done anything to warrant it. It landed hard. He kept it.

### November 2, 2025 — Jackson Stopped Talking to Her
By November 2, 2025, Jackson made the decision to stop talking to Bailey. Not out of anger — out of clarity. She'd called him a shitty person without cause. Her final reason for ending things was "really different morals" and a prior Jansen hookup. His final read: "she's the one who chose how she acted." Full emotional peace. See **active-situations**.

### Nov–Dec 2025 — The Reconnection After Jansen
They'd known each other before, but a prior hookup with Jansen — a distant friend of hers, about a year before they reconnected, meaningless to Jackson, something he regretted — had caused her to drop him initially. They ran into each other at a Thanksgiving party around November 2025. The streak started. Conversations became daily and natural, not forced.

### The Orlando Trip
She went to Orlando and texted him the entire trip. That was the signal — she was choosing to include him even when she didn't have to. The relationship felt real and consistent during this period. She was showing up.

### "I Just Don't Know What I Want"
At some point she pulled back. Said: "I promise it's not washed, I just don't know what I want right now." That sentence captures everything about how the situation ran — she was acknowledging something was there while refusing to commit to it. It wasn't cruelty, it was indecision. But indecision with consequences.

### April 2026 — How It Ended
She cited "really different morals" and the Jansen situation as the reasons. The Jansen thing had happened over a year before they reconnected — it was history she knew about, not a surprise. Jackson didn't argue it. His read at the end: "she's the one who chose how she acted." Not bitterness — clarity. He described the emotional state at closure as "probably the best I've ever felt emotionally." No spiral, no unfinished business. He let it be what it was and moved on clean.

## Mentioned In (Additional)

### January 17, 2026 — Third party relays Bailey's read on Jackson
A mutual contact (rb_waldrup) texted Jackson: "she said you were cute but she's not a fan of your past 😔." This was January 17, 2026 — in the middle of the reconnection window (they rekindled around the Thanksgiving party in Nov 2025). Someone in the mutual circle was actively passing along what Bailey was saying about Jackson behind the scenes. What she communicated was that the attraction was there, but his "past" was the obstacle — which aligns exactly with how it ultimately ended (she cited Jansen as a final reason). The third-party relay confirms this concern was active and circulating among mutual friends well before the final conversation.
Source: **rb_waldrup**

### February 3, 2026 — Harrison's unsolicited opinion in group chat
In the "Robert's funeral 🕊️" group chat, Harrison (harrison.cha7) said "Bailey is like a mosquito bro deadass." He prefaced it with "I sure got a lot to say but I'd rather keep it cordial" — meaning he was actively choosing restraint about her. He had more opinions and held them back. Whatever was happening between Jackson and Bailey in early February 2026, it was registering in the friend circle as a topic worth commenting on.
Source: **Harrison**, **robert's-funeral-🕊️**

## Mentioned In

### October 13, 2025 — first mentioned in Jackson's journal
Jackson wrote about her for the first time, calling her "my wife" and noting she'd said she didn't want a relationship but "she has not seen the absolute 100% JPB." First documented mention of her in any record.
Source: **2025-10-13-1**

### October 19, 2025 — full profile written in journal
He wrote down everything he knew: favorite color purple, flowers daisies/hydrangeas/hibiscus, twin brother Ethan, loves cats, country music, Mexican food, allergic to smoke. Called her "completely wife material" and "I truly admire her." This is the entry where the situation was real to him — detailed enough to catalog. He also wrote "Love Doesn't Exist" in red and then crossed it out.
Source: **2025-10-19-1**

### October 27, 2025 — distance, the Instagram post, and the rejection
She went distant. He posted a photo with another girl on Instagram — a petty move he knew was petty. She rejected him that night, called him a shitty person for "what he did to Jansen." He wrote: "I hate having a bad reputation." Still didn't want to stop pursuing.
Source: **2025-10-27-1**, **2025-10-27-2**

### October 28, 2025 — his defense of the Jansen situation
He worked through it in writing: he'd told Jansen from the start he didn't want a relationship, so why was he the liar? He couldn't reconcile being called a pathological liar for telling the truth. The Jansen dynamic would follow him into the Bailey situation — she used it as her final reason months later.
Source: **2025-10-28-1**

Brittain was the one who made it real by starting the chat.

rows.title: Ella Dupree
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- Snapchat contact from the Auburn era. Significant communication volume.
- No additional biographical detail confirmed beyond Snapchat data.

## Snapchat
- **Username:** elladupree22859
- **Messages:** 412 (Aug 2024 → Mar 2026) — 7th highest all-time
- **Snaps:** 172
- **Friend status:** Unknown (not in friends export — may be unfriended or export gap)
- **Period:** Aug 2024 – Mar 2026 (Auburn era only)

## Significant Moments

### Aug 2024 — How It Started
They connected at Auburn in August 2024 — right at the start of a new school year. Within days they were exchanging snaps, staying up late, telling each other they were obsessed with each other. She told him "I'm glad I know this you" at 3am. He said it might be the hottest thing anyone had ever said to him. It moved fast, the way things do when both people are leaning in at the same time.

### Aug–Sep 2024 — The First Tension: Not Ready
Jackson had just come off the Aubrie breakup. He told Ella Dupree he wasn't ready for anything serious — he needed time to heal. She said she understood but pushed back quietly: "I wanna grow with you." He held firm. The dynamic was charged — real feelings on both sides, but his timing was off. She started getting sharp when frustrated. On August 21, after a night that clearly went sideways, she sent "ok yeah i'm blocking u" — then hours later she was sending photo after photo and then a 4am apology barrage: "I've been bawling my eyes out to Kyra for literally 3 hours bc u mean so so much to me... I can't lose you as a friend... you're one of the only people who actually care." He woke up to all of it and told her he wasn't mad — just tired of her saying mean things when she got upset. He set the boundary clearly: "I really need you right now but you have to start treating me better."

### Oct 2024 — The Cancer Scare
Late one night she texted him scared. She thought she might be sick — thought it might be cancer. "They said this would be the time it would come back," she told him. "What if I die." He stayed on with her. "You're going to be okay, Ella." He didn't brush her off. That she went to him in that moment meant something about how much she trusted him, whatever else was complicated between them.

### Jul 2025 — The Blowup About His Best Friend
By summer 2025 the talking stage had long passed, but she'd apparently been involved with one of his close friends. When he found out, he confronted her directly: "you said i could trust you but literally the entire time ur fking my best friend." She laughed it off — literally typed "LOL / That's hilarious." He made clear it wasn't about exclusivity in principle; it was about the specific person. "Why him — someone I've literally told you about multiple times." She pushed back: "You told me a million times you never wanted anything serious." He didn't argue the logic, he just held his position: "stay delusional twin / it fits you / him too." The conversation ended there. It didn't blow the connection up entirely — they kept snapping through the end of 2025 — but it established a ceiling on trust.

### Nov 2025 — The Bar Night and the Broken Toes
He was out at a bar with Dev Patel and needed a ride. He asked Ella Dupree — described in his journal as an "ex talking stage" — to come get them. She did, but he accidentally left his phone in her car. When she wouldn't return it, he went to her place, barged through the door to get it back, and accidentally broke her toes in the process. She screamed at him. He drove home panicked and sick about it. He called his mom; his mom said he was in the right. He felt terrible regardless. It was the kind of ugly, small-hours incident that doesn't define a relationship but does sit in the record of it. A few days later he came down with a 103–104 fever. That week he wrote: "Day by day life gets harder and I am getting tired."

## Mentioned In

### November 8, 2025 — Discussed with **Angie Pate**
Jackson called his mom to talk through the bar night / broken-toes situation with Ella. Angie said he was in the right. Source: **2025-11-08-1** and **Angie Pate**.

rows.title: Emma Williard
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- Jackson's strongest romantic interest as of Spring 2026. Both from Gadsden, Alabama.
- Previously talked for 6 months in high school before she dated **Frey** for 1.5 years.
- Reconnected March 6, 2026 at a bar — talked 4 hours, Jackson fell asleep at her place.

## Known Details
- **Flowers:** White hydrangeas (reposted hydrangea TikTok — confirmed preference)
- **Rings:** Oval cut (noted for the long game)
- **Movies:** Loves going to the theater
- **Mom:** Knows about Jackson by name. Asked "is Jackson staying with you tonight?" at the rodeo.
- **Snap BSF list:** Jackson is #2 (mom is #1)
- **Communication style:** Not a heavy phone communicator — don't read into response time

## Timeline
- March 6 — Reconnected at a bar. Talked 4 hours. Jackson realized he's in love with her.
- March 9 (her birthday) — Texted happy birthday, flirted about "running off together."
- Late March (bar) — Jackson told her he loves her. She said "I love u too" back.
- Treaty Oak Revival rodeo — spent the night together, danced, he drove her home sober, asked her on a date. She said yes.
- ~April 4 — Invited him to margs with her roommates, ended up at her place, talked for hours, fell asleep together.
- April 9 — Date was planned (The Depot + AMC Classic Auburn 14, white hydrangeas ready). She ghosted before it happened. Last contact Apr 7-8 on Snap. No explanation. Jackson was nothing but good to her the entire time.

## What She Communicated
- Said she wants something but is scared of being hurt.
- Said "I love u too" back unprompted.
- Not unsure about him — was navigating the Frey fallout.

## Jackson on Her
- "I know she could be my wife." The clearest he's ever been about anyone.

## Status
- Ghosted before first date. Date never happened. Reason unknown. Unresolved.

## Snapchat Intelligence
- **Username:** ewilliard09
- **Snaps:** 47 (13 sent / 34 received) -- #24 by snaps
- **Messages:** 12 (Jun 28, 2022 -- Apr 8, 2026)
- **Pattern:** Low volume on Snapchat -- relationship lives elsewhere (in person, iMessage likely)
- **Notable messages:** She sent an address (Jul 2022). March-April 2026 reconnection documented. Last messages Apr 7-8 2026 are deleted.
- **Status:** Ghosted before the April 9 2026 date. Date never happened.
- **ewilliard09**

## Significant Moments

### March 6, 2026 — The Reconnection
They ran into each other at a bar. Neither planned it. They ended up talking for four hours and Jackson fell asleep at her place — just talking, nothing more. By the time he left he knew. He described it later as realizing he's in love with her. This was the moment the whole 2026 arc started from — a years-apart reconnection that didn't feel like reconnecting at all, just like picking up something that was always there.

### Late March, 2026 — "I Love You" at the Bar
At another bar, Jackson told her he loves her. She said "I love u too" back — unprompted, not just returning the sentiment. She'd already said she wants something but is scared of being hurt. This was said and received between two people who both knew something was real. It wasn't a declaration and a response — it was mutual and unforced.

### Treaty Oak Revival, ~Late March 2026 — The Rodeo Night
She told him to find her at the rodeo — she initiated the connection that night. They danced. He drove her home sober. He asked her on a date and she said yes. Her mom already knew his name by this point — had asked Emma "is Jackson staying with you tonight?" — meaning Emma had been talking about him at home before any of this became official. Frey found out about the rodeo and texted Emma: "wow cannot believe ur hanging out without an actual loser right now" and "wait till you fall off before you date him, he's so under me." Emma told Jackson about it herself.

### ~April 4, 2026 — Her Place After Margs
She invited him out with her roommates for margs. It turned into hours at her place. They talked. They fell asleep together. At 3–4am she woke him up to go — she had an 8am advisor appointment. She was the one managing his exit, not letting him just stay indefinitely. Nothing dramatic about the night itself — just the pattern of the whole thing: ending up together naturally, without a plan, and it feeling right. She cared about him enough to track both their schedules.

### April 9, 2026 — The Ghost
She went quiet before the planned first date. No explanation, no fight, nothing. Last contact was Apr 7–8 on Snap — and those messages were deleted. He had white hydrangeas ready. The Depot was the plan. The date never happened. He'd been nothing but good to her the entire time. She'd told him she was scared of being hurt — and then disappeared without giving him the chance to show her she wouldn't be. That's where it sits: unresolved, no closure, no bad memory to point at. Just a door that closed before it opened.

## Mentioned In

### Spring 2026 — Emma and Jackson Talking About Frey
Emma was the one who told Jackson that Frey had been talking badly about him since the summer. She also showed or told Jackson about the rodeo texts Frey sent her. She didn't stay neutral — she was transparent with Jackson about what Frey was doing. That act of disclosure is part of what defined where her loyalty sat, and it's part of what makes the ghost on April 9 sit harder. Referenced in **Trey Frachiseur**'s page (Significant Moments and Mentioned In) and **active-situations** (The Frey Situation).

### April 9, 2026, 2am — Referenced by **Trey Frachiseur** in conversation with Jackson
On the night the date didn't happen, at 2am, Jackson sent Trey a status screenshot and they exchanged multiple messages — all now deleted or unavailable. The content is gone. But the timing makes clear: when Emma went silent and the date fell apart, Jackson's first move was to talk to Trey about it. The conversation happened in the same window as the ghost. That's all the record shows.
Source: **Trey Frachiseur**, **trey_frachiseur**

### August 7, 2025 — Mentioned by **Tre Jackson** in conversation with Jackson
Tre sent Jackson a quote attributed back to him: "i miss her real shit" — jackson p. Tre had heard or seen Jackson say this at some point, clocked it, and saved it long enough to send it back months later. At the time (August 2025) Jackson was not yet in active contact with Emma — this predates the March 2026 reconnection. It shows Jackson had been carrying something about her, probably from their earlier history, and that Tre was paying close enough attention to notice and name it. Reveals: Emma occupied enough space in Jackson's mind that his friends registered it.
Source: **Tre Jackson**, **trej3635**

rows.title: Ethan Arce
rows.content_markdown:
## Who He Is
- Jackson's most trusted person. Lives in Oklahoma.
- The one person Jackson lets closest — even Ethan doesn't know about the "not good enough" fear. Nobody does.

## Similarities to Jackson
- Nearly identical in almost every way — the friendship is built on that mirroring

## Key Differences
- Not religious
- Sees people as beneath him — Jackson is the opposite (sees everyone as better than himself)

## Status
- Close friend, long distance (Oklahoma)

## Snapchat
- **Username:** ethan_arce14
- **Messages:** 268 (Jul 2024 → Apr 2026) — ongoing, active
- **Snaps:** 1,009 — highest for any individual in the dataset
- **Note:** The snap-to-message ratio is notable: 3.8 snaps per message. The friendship runs primarily on visual communication, not text. This mirrors the description of him as the most trusted person — they don't need words.
- **Group chat:** **Fagmophobia** (with mrbradyt07, trej3635 — core group chat, Dec 2024–present, 1,045 group snaps)
- **Relationship Map** | **Key Moments**

## Snapchat Intelligence
- **Username:** ethan_arce14
- **Snaps:** 1,042 (604 sent / 438 received) -- #4 all-time, SENDER-dominant
- **Messages:** 268 (Jul 2024 -- Apr 9 2026)
- **Pattern:** One of the ONLY relationships where Jackson sends more snaps than he receives. Initiator with Ethan.
- **Group chats shared:** **brady-gyatt** (with Brady + dbrackett89), **fagmophobia**
- **Status:** Active. Messages as recent as yesterday.
- **ethan_arce14**

## Significant Moments

### Ongoing — The Thing Nobody Knows
Jackson's biggest fear — not being good enough, trying everything and still not being enough — is something he's never told anyone. The person he's closest to, the person he trusts more than anyone else, is Ethan. And even Ethan doesn't know. That's not a failure of the friendship — it's a description of how deep that fear is buried. Ethan is as close as Jackson lets anyone get, and the fear still doesn't come out. The friendship is real and deep. The wall is just deeper.

### July 15, 2025 — "I don't understand why you're so dry with me now"
At 6:20am, Jackson sent Ethan: "like i dont understand why your so dry w me now." It's a rare moment of Jackson expressing something vulnerable directly — he doesn't usually do that. This is the person he trusts most, and he still felt the need to ask why. The message going out at 6am means it was sitting on him. Whatever the distance was — temporary friction, Ethan's own life pulling him away — it was enough for Jackson to say something. The fact that he did says more about what Ethan means to him than anything else in the record.

### January 6, 2025 — The Rap Lyrics
Jackson sent Ethan a rap built around the three of them — Jackson, Ethan, Tre — set in Auburn, positioned as kings. The lyrics are ridiculous but intentional: they're a tribute in the form of a joke. The choice to send something like that at 1:52am to the person you trust most is a specific kind of affection. Jackson doesn't perform closeness, so when he does something like this, it means it.

### November 23, 2025 — "Ur so fucking hot — Maddie"
Ethan sent a message at 2:58am that just said "Ur so fucking hot" and then immediately followed with "– Maddie," meaning his girlfriend had grabbed his phone. It's a small thing. But it's the kind of thing that only happens in a friendship where there's no performance — where the other person's girlfriend feels easy enough around the dynamic to be funny about it, and where nobody needs to explain the joke.

## Group Chat Record

### "gruesome twosome" (March 2025 – April 2026)
Ethan is one of two named members in a group called "gruesome twosome" alongside Tre Jackson (trej3635), with Jackson as the third. 55 messages, March 2025 through April 3, 2026. Readable lines are sparse: Ethan sent "dude what the hell" (May 31, 2025), and "i was stuck" (Jan 4, 2026), and "🔥🔥" (April 3, 2026). Most content is snaps and unavailable notes. The name "gruesome twosome" almost certainly refers to Ethan and Tre — they're the twosome. The group is separate from Fagmophobia, suggesting these two communicate in multiple channels with Jackson.

### "Foursome of d00m" (April–May 2025)
Ethan and Tre appear together with Jackson in this group as well. Ethan's readable contribution: "Cucumber Calvin" (April 15, 2025 — an inside joke or nickname) and a photo April 22, 2025.

## Mentioned In (Additional)

### December 28, 2025 — "Very natty 💉" group chat
Ethan appeared in a two-message group chat called "Very natty 💉" on December 28, 2025 alongside mrbradyt07 — both sent snaps. The group name is a reference to natural bodybuilding or gym culture ("natty" = no steroids, the syringe emoji ironic). December 28 was the day after Jackson's birthday (Dec 27). The group places Ethan in contact with mrbradyt07 (the same person who appears in the Fagmophobia and Racist mfers group chats with Tre Jackson), confirming overlap in social circles between Ethan and Tre's world even from Oklahoma.
Source: **very-natty-💉**

## Mentioned In

### April 6–17, 2025 — "Lads" Group Chat with coben_guest23
Ethan appeared in a group chat called "Lads" alongside coben_guest23 (Coben Guest, a recurring figure from Jackson's high school soccer circle). On April 6, Ethan sent a sticker. On April 17, coben_guest23 sent a photo. Sparse content, but the group confirms Ethan and Coben had at least some overlap in Jackson's social world during spring 2025 — despite Ethan being in Oklahoma. Most likely a legacy group from a shared in-person period that was still technically alive.
Source: **lads**

### April 6, 2026 — discussed by Jackson in personal reflections journal
Jackson wrote a dedicated section about Ethan in his personal reflections. Called him the most trusted person in his life, noted he lives in Oklahoma, and spelled out the key similarities and differences: nearly identical in almost every way, except Ethan isn't religious and sees people as beneath him while Jackson sees everyone as better than himself. The most significant line: "Even Ethan doesn't know about the 'not good enough' fear. Nobody does." Jackson disclosed this to the record, not to Ethan himself. The trust is real, and so is the distance that exists even within it.
Source: **2026-personal-reflections**

### December 2021 — mentioned by **Anna** in conversation with Jackson
When Jackson asked Anna why she readded him after 31 weeks of no contact, she explained directly: "bc me and E broke up and ur my friend." She followed up that she "wasn't allowed to talk to any of my guy friends" during the relationship — meaning Ethan had controlled who she could talk to, and the moment they broke up, she immediately reconnected with Jackson. Jackson called it "kind of restrictive" and she replied "you live and u learn." Reveals Ethan as having been controlling in at least one prior relationship — controlling enough that Anna deleted Jackson from her contact list while they were together, and reconnected the same day the relationship ended.
Source: **Anna**, **atallent97**

Jackson figured out on his own that Evan and Riley had broken up — no one told him, he just noticed she hadn't been to church with Evan or going to his car anymore. He messaged at 4am to ask if it was true. Evan confirmed it, said they kept having problems they couldn't solve and it kept getting worse. When Jackson asked if they'd get back together, Evan said "idk" and signed off quickly. Jackson's response before they ended the conversation: "hope u doing good." Small moment, but it shows how Jackson moves — pays attention, shows up, doesn't make it weird.

### June 2023 — Evan Checks In Back
Jackson messaged Evan saying he'd cut himself and was sorry for the slow response. Evan's immediate reply was just "Are you ok?" Short, no elaboration — but that response matters. The friendship is built on that kind of low-key, present-when-it-counts energy on both sides.

Jackson got a new one-handed bowling ball and came to Evan for advice on getting it to hook more. Evan was the team's social glue — the one who organized practices, called out team issues, and pushed for accountability.

rows.title: Ivonne Joiner
rows.content_markdown:
## Who She Is
- CRNP (Certified Registered Nurse Practitioner) at Rainbow City Family Medicine, Gadsden, Alabama.
- Jackson's provider for the February 25, 2026 visit that became the center of the academic integrity case.

## The Academic Integrity Situation
- Evaluated and treated Jackson on 02/25/2026 for nausea. Prescribed Zofran (ondansetron).
- The clinic generated an excuse note on 02/24 (phone call date) before Jackson's actual visit — a standard clerical practice.
- A corrected note was emailed to Jackson on 02/25 at 12:21 PM, confirming the 02/25 visit date.
- When Scott Miller contacted the clinic, Ivonne Joiner and the front desk told him Jackson had been seen on 02/25 — accurate. Miller interpreted this as evasion.
- The pharmacy record for Zofran (filled 02/25) serves as the independent third-party proof of the visit — cannot be backdated or altered.

## Role in Jackson's Defense
- Key witness. Her name is on both the prescription and the corrected clinic note.
- The virtual visit confirmation email (02/25, 9:37 AM, listing Ivonne Joiner, CNP) corroborates Jackson's full account.

The morning of Jackson's nursing program interview, Trey texted him: "Good luck on your interview today big dog. You gonna kill that shit." At 6am, Trey sent a running narrative of a dream he'd just woken from: they were in a mountain town, Trey stole back Jackson's fake ID from someone who took it, a guy started chasing them, Jackson physically carried Trey to the car when his legs gave out, four cops got on them, they kept going because Jackson had vapes, they slid off the road and crashed but everyone lived. Kennadie was there the whole time. Ended with: "Then I woke up." The fact that he texted this out unprompted at 6am — and that the dream had both of them in a loyalty-under-fire scenario — captures what the friendship looked like at its most natural.

At 2am on April 9 — the night Emma was supposed to go on a first date with Jackson and ghosted instead — Jackson sent Trey a status screenshot and they exchanged multiple messages that are now deleted or unavailable. The content is gone. But Jackson went to Trey when things went sideways that night. Whatever got said, Trey was who he reached first.

When Emma went to the Treaty Oak Revival rodeo with Jackson, Frey found out. He texted her: "wow cannot believe ur hanging out without an actual loser right now" and "wait till you fall off before you date him, he's so under me." Emma told Jackson about it. He's been talking badly about Jackson since the summer — Emma confirmed this directly, separately from the rodeo texts. The texts revealed something specific: Frey felt threatened enough to try to poison the relationship before it started. Jackson's response wasn't confrontation. He's living in the same apartment as this person, planning to room with him next year too, and not reacting. The conversation between them is coming eventually. He's not forcing it.
